entry.title: Changing the Fiscal Year Variant in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition
entry.content:
What You Need to Know Before Requesting a Fiscal Period Variant Change

Changing the fiscal year definition in a productive SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition system is a rare but critical requirement—typically driven by major business events such as company sales, mergers, demergers, or acquisitions.

This blog post explains why fiscal year variant changes are complex, what scenarios are supported, and how customers can proceed safely and compliantly using SAP’s recommended approach.

Business Context: Why Companies Need to Change Their Fiscal Year Variant

Customers usually request a fiscal year or fiscal period change when new business conditions arise, for example:

  • Sale of a company or business unit
  • Merger or acquisition
  • Division or carve‑out of an existing entity
  • Alignment with a new group reporting calendar

These changes often impact how financial reporting periods are structured—and therefore require changes to the fiscal year variant.

Common Scenarios

  1. Introducing a Shortened Fiscal Year

A typical example is a transition from a calendar fiscal year (January–December) to a non‑calendar fiscal year (July–June).

In this case:

  • The old fiscal year ends in December
  • The new fiscal year starts in July
  • A shortened fiscal year is required to bridge the period January–June

This avoids gaps or overlaps in financial reporting.

  1. Changing Fiscal Posting Period Definitions

Another common requirement is changing posting period structures, for example:

  • From calendar months to a 4‑4‑5 calendar
  • Or to 13 posting periods based on four‑week cycles

Although the fiscal year start/end dates may remain the same, the fiscal period definition must be adjusted accordingly.

Why Fiscal Year Variants Cannot Be Changed Directly in Production

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, it is not possible to change the fiscal year variant retrospectively in a productive system.

The key reasons are:

🔹Data Integrity and Dependencies

Fiscal year information is deeply embedded in financial data such as:

  • Journal entries
  • Actuals and planning data
  • Universal Journal tables (for example, ACDOCA, ACDOCP)

Changing the fiscal year definition directly would risk inconsistencies in existing transaction data.

🔹Cross‑Application Impact

The fiscal year variant is used across multiple application areas:

  • General Ledger Accounting
  • Asset Accounting
  • Controlling
  • Reporting

Any change affects the entire financial landscape, not just one configuration object.

🔹Customer‑Specific Complexity

Each customer has a unique setup:

  • Ledger assignments
  • Reporting requirements
  • Audit constraints

This makes a standardized, self‑service change technically and operationally infeasible.

The Supported Approach: SAP DMLT Consulting Service

For SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, changing the fiscal year definition or fiscal period structure requires a customer‑specific DMLT (Data Management & Landscape Transformation) consulting Service.

The consulting service typically includes:

  • Impact analysis
  • Configuration of the new fiscal year variant
  • Data conversion where required
  • Support for customer‑specific execution steps
  • Coordination with audit and compliance requirements

This applies whether you:

  • Introduce a shortened fiscal year, or
  • Change only the fiscal period (posting period) definition

Important Scope and Restrictions

Please consider the following constraints when planning:

  • ✅Only one single fiscal year variant assigned across all ledgers and company codes can be changed
  • ✅Supported only for SAP Central Business Configuration–managed implementations
  • ✅The change impacts future data only
  • ❌Historical transaction data is out of scope

Technical Note

If a preconfigured fiscal year variant was used during the initial implementation:

  • A new customer‑specific fiscal year variant ID must be created
  • All data containing the old variant ID (for example, line items in ACDOCA) must be converted as part of the project

How to Request the Change

To initiate the process:

  1. Plan early and ensure sufficient lead time
  2. Align with internal stakeholders and your auditor
  3. Create a case under component CA‑LT‑SRV‑2CL
  4. Engage with SAP DMLT Consulting Service to define scope, timeline, and execution strategy

Early engagement significantly reduces risk and helps ensure a smooth transition.

Key Takeaways

  • Changing a fiscal year in SAP S/4HANA Cloud is not a configuration activity, but a controlled transformation
  • Shortened fiscal years and fiscal period changes are fully supported—with expert guidance
  • Early planning, audit alignment, and SAP DMLT involvement are critical for success
  • 3678328 - Change of Fiscal Year Variant in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

If you have gone through a fiscal year change in SAP S/4HANA Cloud or are planning one, feel free to share your experience or questions in the comments. Collaboration and shared learnings make these complex topics easier for everyone.

entry.title: Re: Unable to perform Support Marketplace cleanup ( Dupli...
entry.content:
As per outlined in 1843303 - How to delete On-premise systems - SAP for Me , the following S-user authorizations are required in order to delete on-premise systems: 

  • Display System Data (required to view system data)
  • Edit System Data (required to edit or maintain system data)

Below are the steps to delete on‑premise systems:

Step 1: Close open support cases (for all users) for the system to be deleted. Tip: See KBA 1812813 for instructions on searching open cases.

Important: For Sybase, you must use the check-in option in the License Keys Request application instead (see KBA 2639679) This ensures the licenses are added back to the pool of available licenses.

Step 2: Delete the system(s) as follows:

  1. Open the System Data application in SAP for Me and find the system to be deleted

    • On the On Premise Systems card, under SYSTEMS, click on the System number link
    • Click on the System Number link again top left (or click Delete on the top right)

  2. In the System details page, click Delete (top right)

  3. When prompted, click Delete System

If you have any questions or need further assistance, please feel free to contact us. 

Best regards,
Judy 
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Cloud ALM User
entry.content:
Your setup is very common and you're on the right track:

Manual user creation in Cloud ALM — This doesn't connect to your corporate identity system at all. Every time someone joins, leaves, or changes roles, you'd need to update Cloud ALM manually. It works for a quick test, but in a real project it becomes a headache fast.

Assigning roles via the BTP Cockpit — This is the most common approach in smaller projects because it's straightforward. Your users already exist in the identity system, so you just assign them the right roles. The problem is that nothing is automated — if someone leaves the company, you have to remember to remove their access in BTP manually. That's a compliance risk.

Automated provisioning via groups — This is what most mature projects use. The idea is simple: you connect your corporate identity system to Cloud ALM through a provisioning service, and it handles everything automatically. When someone joins a team, they get access. When they leave the company, their access is removed. You don't have to think about it. That's the main reason this approach is recommended — it's not just about convenience, it's about making sure access is always in sync with reality.

Since your users are already flowing from your corporate identity system into the SAP identity layer, you're most of the way there. The missing piece is just connecting that flow all the way into Cloud ALM with automated group-based provisioning.

entry.title: Re: Create purchase requisition for Unplanned components...
entry.content: Automatic PR creation for newly added components requires a re-run of MRP (either single-item or plant-wide). Without this, the system will not recognize the new demand. If automation is critical, you can configure a background MRP run or use BAdIs/Enhancements to trigger PR creation immediately upon BOM update.

entry.title: Re: DSD Settlement
entry.content:
Manual Settlement
The sales representative or back-office user initiates settlement explicitly. After completing the route, they review all deliveries, returns, and payments collected. Using the settlement transaction, they manually confirm quantities, prices, and cash received. Once confirmed, the system posts the financial documents (e.g., customer invoice, cash journal entry).
Manual settlement is typically used when you want control and verification before posting, for example, if discrepancies are common or if cash handling needs extra oversight.

Automatic Settlement
Settlement is triggered automatically at the end of the route or based on predefined rules. The system consolidates deliveries, returns, and payments without user intervention. Financial postings (billing, FI entries) are generated immediately. Automatic settlement is useful when processes are standardized and trusted, reducing administrative overhead. It is often scheduled as a background job so that once a route is closed, settlement runs automatically.

In short:

  • Manual settlement = user-driven, with checks before posting.
  • Automatic settlement = system-driven, faster, but requires confidence in data accuracy.

entry.title: Product Substitution :Controlling Substitution Priority Independent of Material Creation Date
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I have a requirement to configure product substitution from one product to multiple alternative products in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

The scenario is as follows:
Product A (original product) should be substituted by Products B, C, and D when A is not available (i.e., no stock exists for A in the system).

Currently, the system allows sequential substitution (A B C D), where it checks stock availability at each level and selects the next available product. If limited stock is available for a product, the system partially confirms and continues checking the next alternative.

However, my requirement is slightly different:

  • Product A should first be substituted by Product B.
  • If B has no stock, Product C should be considered.
  • If C also has no stock, the system should always confirm Product D, regardless of stock quantity (i.e., not dependent on availability checks like the previous materials).

Please consider the following scenarios:

Scenario 1:
Order quantity = 20

  • Product A has 5 units in stock system confirms 5 units of A
  • System then checks Product B if B has 10 units, confirm 10 units
  • System then checks Product C if no stock, proceed to D
  • Product D supplies the remaining 5 units

Scenario 2:
Order quantity = 20

  • Products A, B, and C have no stock
  • Product D has 20 units → system confirms full quantity (20 units) from D

Material Creation Dates:

  • Product A – 01.04.2026
  • Product B – 03.04.2026
  • Product C – 07.04.2026
  • Product D – 06.04.2026

Currently, the system prioritizes materials based on creation date, which leads to unintended behavior. For example, Product D is considered before Product C due to its earlier creation date. As a result, the substitution sequence becomes A B D C instead of the desired order.

Additionally, after material confirmation, the system determines the batch based on the FIFO rule.

I would like to understand

  • Is there a way to achieve the required substitution logic within standard product substitution functionality?
  • Alternatively, is there another approach to fulfill this requirement?

This is a critical customer requirement for a Public Cloud go-live.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Regards,

Ashish

entry.title: Rethinking SAP Architecture in an AI-First World
entry.content:
SAP systems have traditionally been built for predictability, based on structured data, defined processes and clear outcomes. But AI introduces a different paradigm: systems that are context-driven, adaptive and not always deterministic.

This raises an important question: Is our current SAP architecture ready for this shift?

In this article, I explore how concepts like context layers, semantic understanding and identity-aware intelligence are becoming increasingly relevant in modern SAP landscapes. This shift is not just about adding new capabilities on top of existing systems. It has implications on how SAP landscapes are structured and how different layers interact with each other.

To understand this better, it is useful to look at how traditional SAP systems differ from AI-driven systems.

Deterministic Systems vs Probabilistic Systems

Traditional SAP systems follow a clear pattern:

  • Define process
  • Structure data
  • Assign roles
  • Execute transactions

AI systems, on the other hand:

  • interpret context
  • retrieve information dynamically
  • generate responses based on probability

This creates a gap between how systems are built and how they are now expected to behave.

 

Key Architectural Shifts

  • Shift from Structured Data to Context Layers

SAP systems are highly optimized for structured data. However AI-driven interactions require more than structured records. They require contextual understanding, often derived from:

  • documents (policies, contracts)
  • knowledge bases
  • historical interactions

This introduces the need for a context retrieval layer, where vector-based retrieval mechanisms complement traditional data models.

 

  • Shift from Defined Relationships to Semantic Understanding

In SAP, relationships are explicitly modeled:
employee → position → manager → cost center.

AI systems extend beyond these predefined relationships. They rely on semantic connections, such as:

  • understanding similar concepts across different terminologies
  • linking information across modules without rigid joins

This is where knowledge graphs play a key role, enabling systems to represent meaning rather than just structure.

 

  • Shift from Role-Based Access to Identity-Aware Intelligence

Identity in traditional SAP landscapes is primarily about:

  • authentication
  • role-based authorization

In AI-driven architectures, identity becomes part of the decision context.

The system must determine not only:

  • what data a user can access

but also:

  • what the AI is allowed to retrieve, infer, and respond with for that specific user

This makes identity services critical for ensuring trust, compliance, and controlled AI behavior.

 

  • Shift from UI-Driven Interaction to Intent-Driven Interaction

SAP user experience has evolved significantly over time. However, the bigger shift is not just UI improvement, but interaction model transformation.

Users are increasingly moving from:

  • navigating applications
    to
  • expressing intent

For example:

  • instead of opening multiple apps to apply leave
  • users may simply ask a system to perform the action

This introduces an AI-driven interaction layer that orchestrates actions across underlying systems.

Rather than replacing existing systems, these capabilities tend to form additional layers around them, as illustrated below.

 

 

Example: SuccessFactors Scenario

Consider a simple leave related query in a SuccessFactors environment:

A user asks: “Can I take leave next week?”

To respond meaningfully, the system may need to:

  • retrieve structured employee data (leave balance, approvals)
  • interpret policy documents (leave rules)
  • consider user-specific context (role, geography, exceptions)

This goes beyond traditional transactional processing and requires combining:

  • structured data
  • unstructured content
  • contextual identity

Such scenarios highlight why additional architectural layers are becoming necessary.

 

Wrapping up...

The role of SAP architecture is gradually expanding.

Beyond designing processes and data models, there is now a growing need to design:

  • context-aware systems
  • semantically connected information
  • identity-aware intelligence layers

This represents an evolving shift from traditional system design toward more adaptive and intelligent architectures.

It is still early but the direction is becoming clearer.

entry.title: Re: Down Payment adjustment for Partial delivery/Partial...
entry.content: There is no standard SAP S/4HANA On-Premise 2023 functionality that automatically prorates and adjusts customer down payments against partial invoices in scope item BKJ. The standard down payment process only clears the advance against the final invoice or when you manually apply it. To achieve partial allocation, you need either a custom enhancement (e.g., service material + condition type) or manual clearing in FI. A custom condition type/service material approach is simpler, configuration-driven, or a BAdI enhancement to prorate down payments across partial invoices (more complex, but seamless). If your business strongly requires proportional down payment adjustment, I recommend raising this as a requirement to SAP via OSS note/incident, as it is a known limitation in standard BKJ.

entry.title: Database Operations Through AMDP
entry.content:
Introduction: AMDP (ABAP Managed Database Procedures) is a framework in SAP that allows ABAP developers to write and execute database-specific SQL Script code (mainly for SAP HANA) directly from ABAP classes. It gives ABAP the ability to run complex, high-performance database operations on HANA using SQL Script, instead of pulling data into ABAP memory.

INSERT (Adding Data):
Use the INSERT statement to add new rows to a table. You can insert single rows or multiple
rows using INSERT INTO ... SELECT

METHOD insert_data
  BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB
  LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
  USING ZPS_TEST.

    IF NOT EXISTS ( SELECT *
                      FROM ZPS_TEST
                     WHERE eno = :e_no )
    THEN
      INSERT INTO ZPS_TEST VALUES (
          session_context('CLIENT'),
          :e_no,
          :e_name,
          session_context('SAP_SYSTEM_DATE'),
          session_context('APPLICATIONUSER'),
          TO_VARCHAR(CURRENT_TIME, 'HH24MISS'),
          session_context('APPLICATIONUSER'),
          TO_VARCHAR(CURRENT_TIME, 'HH24MISS')
      );

      i_msg = 'Success';
    ELSE
      i_msg = 'Unsuccessful';
    END IF;

  ENDMETHOD.

UPDATE (Modifying Data):
Use the UPDATE statement to modify existing rows in a table. Use the WHERE clause to
specify which rows to update. Be careful not to omit the WHERE clause, as that would
update all rows in the table.

METHOD update_data
    BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB
    LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
    USING ZPS_TEST.

    IF EXISTS ( SELECT * FROM ZPS_TEST WHERE eno = :e_no ) THEN

      UPDATE ZPS_TEST SET
          ename   = :e_name,
          euperson = session_context('APPLICATIONUSER'),
          eutime   = TO_VARCHAR(CURRENT_TIME, 'HH24MISS')
        WHERE eno = :e_no;

      i_msg = 'Success';
    ELSE
      i_msg = 'Unsuccessful';
    END IF;

  ENDMETHOD.

DELETE (Removing Data):
Use the DELETE statement to remove rows from a table. As with UPDATE, use the WHERE
clause carefully to avoid deleting unintended rows.

METHOD delete_data
    BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB
    LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
    USING ZPS_TEST.

    IF EXISTS ( SELECT * FROM ZPS_TEST WHERE eno = :e_no ) THEN

      DELETE FROM ZPS_TEST
        WHERE eno = :e_no;

      i_msg = 'Success';
    ELSE
      i_msg = 'Unsuccessful';
    END IF;

  ENDMETHOD.

Program to Call Above Methods in ABAP:

REPORT zrj_amdp_emp_crud.
PARAMETERS : i_ono TYPE zde_ono OBLIGATORY.
PARAMETERS : i_name TYPE zde_ename MODIF ID a1.

PARAMETERS pr1 TYPE c RADIOBUTTON GROUP r1 DEFAULT 'X'.
PARAMETERS pr2 TYPE c RADIOBUTTON GROUP r1.
PARAMETERS pr3 TYPE c RADIOBUTTON GROUP r1.

DATA : lv_insert  TYPE string.
DATA : lv_update  TYPE string.
DATA : lv_delete  TYPE string.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  IF pr1 = 'X'.
    zps_c_emp=>insert_data(
        EXPORTING
            e_no = i_ono
            e_name = i_name
        IMPORTING
            i_msg = lv_insert
            ).

    IF lv_insert = 'Success' .
      WRITE : TEXT-001 , i_ono.
    ELSE.
      WRITE : TEXT-002 .
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

  IF pr2 = 'X'.
    zps_c_emp=>delete_data(
        EXPORTING
            e_no = i_ono
        IMPORTING
            i_msg = lv_update
            ).

    IF lv_update = 'Success' .
      WRITE : TEXT-003 , i_ono.
    ELSE.
      WRITE : TEXT-004 .
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

  IF pr3 = 'X'.
    zps_c_emp=>update_data(
        EXPORTING
            e_no = i_ono
            e_name = i_name
        IMPORTING
            i_msg = lv_delete
            ).

    IF lv_delete = 'Success' .
      WRITE : TEXT-005 , i_ono.
    ELSE.
      WRITE : TEXT-006 .
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  IF pr3 ='X'.
    LOOP AT SCREEN.
      IF screen-group1 = 'A1'.
        screen-active = 0.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDIF.

Conclusion:
AMDP allows ABAP developers to perform powerful database operations directly in SAP HANA using SQL Script. By embedding SQL Script inside ABAP classes, AMDP enables fast execution of SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE operations at the database level, avoiding data transfer to the ABAP layer.

entry.title: Cost Difference Not Posted Automatically at Subcontracting Goods Receipt (FERT, Standard Cost)
entry.content:
Dear Support Team,

We would like to know if the cost difference can be posted at Subcontracting Goods Receipt. Please refer to the content as follows:

(1) System Information:

System: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
Business Scenario: Subcontracting process (HW business)
Material Type: FERT (Finished Product) and ROH (Raw Material) and HALB (Semi-Finished Goods)
Price Control: S (Standard Price) for FERT, V (Average Price) for ROH and HALB

(2) Background:
In our subcontracting scenario, we are using standard cost for finished products (FERT).We would like to understand whether the system can automatically capture and post the cost difference between:

Standard cost of the finished product
Actual cost derived from component consumption and subcontracting service

(3) Test Scenario:

We executed the following test:

Finished Product: FERT (Standard Price = 1,500,000 JPY)
Subcontracting Purchase Order: 1 JPY
Components: HALB / ROH consumed as per BOM

Step 1: Goods Receipt (Subcontracting Inbound)
System posted:

Dr Inventory (Finished Product): 1,500,000 JPY
Cr COGS (50300000): 1,500,000 JPY
Dr Subcontracting Service (Dummy): 1 JPY
Cr GR/IR: 1 JPY
Component consumption postings were also generated (HALB / ROH → COGS / consumption accounts)

Step 2: Supplier Invoice (SIV)
System posted:

Dr GR/IR: 1 JPY
Cr Accounts Payable: 1 JPY

Observed Result:

GR/IR and AP postings are correctly generated based on PO amount
Inventory is posted at standard price
Component consumption is posted correctly

However:

No posting is generated to capture the difference between:

Standard cost (1,500,000 JPY)
Actual cost (components + subcontracting service)

(4) Expected Behavior / Question:

We would like to confirm:

Is it possible in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to automatically post the cost difference at the time of Goods Receipt for subcontracting?
> If yes:
What configuration or scope item is required?
Can this be controlled via account determination (e.g., GBB/BSX) or other settings?
> If not:
Is this behavior standard system design?
Is the only supported approach to capture cost differences via Material Ledger / actual costing at period-end?
Are there any alternative standard solutions to capture this variance automatically (e.g., separate posting logic, enhancement, or best practice)?

Additional Note:
We are currently not using Material Ledger / actual costing.

Request:
Please confirm whether this behavior is standard and advise if any standard functionality exists to fulfill this requirement.

Thank you for your support.

Best Regards,

Michi

entry.title: Deep Entity Handling in SAP UI5 Using OData Service
entry.content:
• Introduction:

A Deep Entity allows you to send and receive hierarchical data (parent + child) in a single request, making your application more efficient, cleaner, and closer to real business scenarios.

In this blog, we will build a complete end-to-end example of a  Deep Entity, covering:

  • Backend implementation using SEGW (ABAP OData)
  • Frontend implementation using SAP UI5
  • Read (Expand) and Create (Deep Insert) operations

Architecture Overview:

SAP UI5 App 

   ↓ 

OData Service (Gateway) 

   ↓ 

Backend Logic (ABAP) 

   ↓ 

Database Tables 

Backend Implementation (ABAP - OData Service):

Step 1: Define Entities in SEGW:

Create: 

  • Header Entity → Sales Order (VBAK) 

  • Item Entity → Sales Order Item (VBAP) 

Step 2: Create Association :

  • Association: Header → Item (1: N)  

  • Navigation Property: znavigation.

Step 3: Create Deep Structure: 

  • Go To: MPC EXT → Source Code-Based. 
  • Create Deep Structure.

 

class ZCL_ZK_DEEP_ENTITY_MPC_EXT definition 
   public 
   inheriting from ZCL_ZK_DEEP_ENTITY_MPC 
   create public . 
public section. 
  types: 
     BEGIN OF ty_deep_str, 
               vbeln              TYPE  vbeln_va, 
               erdat              TYPE  erdat, 
               ernam              TYPE  ernam, 
               auart              TYPE  auart, 
               kunnr              TYPE  kunnr, 
               znavigation TYPE TABLE OF zcl_zk_deep_entity_mpc_ext=>ts_vbap WITH DEFAULT KEY, 
             END OF ty_deep_str . 
protected section. 
   PRIVATE SECTION. 
ENDCLASS. 

CLASS ZCL_ZK_DEEP_ENTITY_MPC_EXT IMPLEMENTATION. 
ENDCLASS.

 

 Step 3: Redefine GET_EXPANDED_ENTITY, GET_EXPANDED_ENTITYSET, CREATE_DEEP_ENTITY. 

Go to: 

DPC_EXT Class → Method: 

Method: /IWBEP/IF_MGW_APPL_SRV_RUNTIME~GET_EXPANDED_ENTITY. 

 

METHOD /iwbep/if_mgw_appl_srv_runtime~get_expanded_entity. 
 
  DATA: ls_deep_str TYPE zcl_zk_deep_entity_mpc_ext=>ty_deep_str, 
         ls_vbak     TYPE zstr_vbak, 
         lt_vbap     TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zstr_vbap. 
 
  DATA lv_vbeln TYPE vbeln_va. 
 
  "-------------------------------------------------- 
   " Read key 
   "-------------------------------------------------- 
   lv_vbeln = VALUE #( it_key_tab[ name = 'Vbeln' ]-value OPTIONAL ). 
   lv_vbeln = |{ lv_vbeln ALPHA = IN }|. 
 
  "-------------------------------------------------- 
   " Read Header (VBAK) 
   "-------------------------------------------------- 
   SELECT SINGLE 
          vbeln, 
          erdat, 
          ernam, 
          auart, 
          kunnr 
     FROM zdt_vbak 
     INTO _vbak 
     WHERE vbeln = _vbeln. 
 
  IF sy-subrc <> 0. 
     RETURN. 
   ENDIF. 
 
  "-------------------------------------------------- 
   " Read Items (VBAP) 
   "-------------------------------------------------- 
   SELECT 
          vbeln, 
          posnr, 
          matnr, 
          kwmeng, 
          vrkme 
     FROM zdt_vbap 
     INTO TABLE _vbap 
     WHERE vbeln = _vbeln. 
 
  "-------------------------------------------------- 
   " Explicit Header Mapping (IMPORTANT) 
   "-------------------------------------------------- 
   CLEAR ls_deep_str. 
 
  ls_deep_str-vbeln = ls_vbak-vbeln. 
   ls_deep_str-erdat = ls_vbak-erdat. 
   ls_deep_str-ernam = ls_vbak-ernam. 
   ls_deep_str-auart = ls_vbak-auart. 
   ls_deep_str-kunnr = ls_vbak-kunnr. 
 
  "-------------------------------------------------- 
   " Items Mapping 
   "-------------------------------------------------- 
   IF lt_vbap IS NOT INITIAL. 
     ls_deep_str-znavigation = CORRESPONDING #( lt_vbap ). 
   ENDIF. 
 
  "-------------------------------------------------- 
   " Return Deep Entity 
   "-------------------------------------------------- 
   copy_data_to_ref( 
     EXPORTING 
       is_data = ls_deep_str 
     CHANGING 
       cr_data = er_entity 
   ). 
 
  APPEND 'znavigation' TO et_expanded_clauses. 
 
ENDMETHOD.

 

Method: /IWBEP/IF_MGW_APPL_SRV_RUNTIME~GET_EXPANDED_ENTITYSET. 

 

  method /IWBEP/IF_MGW_APPL_SRV_RUNTIME~GET_EXPANDED_ENTITYSET. 
 
      DATA : lt_vbak TYPE ZCL_ZK_DEEP_ENTITY_MPC_EXT=>tt_vbak, 
              lt_vbap TYPE ZCL_ZK_DEEP_ENTITY_MPC_EXT=>tt_vbap, 
              ls_deep type ZCL_ZK_DEEP_ENTITY_MPC_EXT=>ty_deep_str, 
              lt_deep TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ZCL_ZK_DEEP_ENTITY_MPC_EXT=>ty_deep_str. 
 
 
        SELECT FROM zdt_vbak FIELDS VBELN, 
                                     ERDAT, 
                                     ERNAM, 
                                     AUART, 
                                     KUNNR INTO TABLE _vbak. 
 
          LOOP AT lt_vbak ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_vbak>). 

           clear  ls_deep . 
 
            ls_deep = CORRESPONDING #( <fs_vbak> ). 
 
            SELECT FROM zdt_vbap FIELDS VBELN, 
                                         POSNR, 
                                         MATNR, 
                                         KWMENG, 
                                         VRKME 
               WHERE vbeln = @<fs_vbak>-vbeln 
               INTO TABLE _vbap. 
 
              ls_deep-znavigation =  CORRESPONDING #( lt_vbak ). 
 
             APPEND ls_deep to lt_deep. 
 
          ENDLOOP. 

          copy_data_to_ref( 
             EXPORTING 
               is_data = lt_deep 
             CHANGING 
               cr_data = er_entityset 
           ). 

          APPEND 'znavigation' to et_expanded_clauses. 

  endmethod.

 

 Method: /IWBEP/IF_MGW_APPL_SRV_RUNTIME~CREATE_DEEP_ENTITY. 

 

  METHOD /iwbep/if_mgw_appl_srv_runtime~create_deep_entity. 
     DATA : ls_deep    TYPE zcl_zk_deep_entity_mpc_ext=>ty_deep_str, 
            ls_vbak    TYPE zcl_zk_deep_entity_mpc_ext=>ts_vbak, 
            ls_vbap    TYPE zcl_zk_deep_entity_mpc_ext=>ts_vbap, 
 
           ls_vbak2   TYPE zdt_vbak, 
            ls_vbap2   TYPE zdt_vbap, 
 
           lv_vbeln   TYPE zdt_vbak-vbeln, 
            lv_posnr   TYPE zdt_vbap-posnr, 
 
           lv_vbeln_n TYPE zdt_vbak-vbeln, 
            lv_posnr_n TYPE zdt_vbap-posnr. 
 
    io_data_provider->read_entry_data( 
       IMPORTING 
         es_data = ls_deep 
     ). 
 
    SELECT MAX( vbeln ) 
           INTO _vbeln 
           FROM zdt_vbak. 
 
    IF lv_vbeln IS INITIAL. 
       lv_vbeln_n = 1. 
     ELSE. 
       lv_vbeln_n = lv_vbeln + 1. 
     ENDIF. 
 
    lv_vbeln_n = |{ lv_vbeln_n ALPHA = IN }|. 
     ls_vbak-vbeln = lv_vbeln_n. 
     ls_vbak-erdat = sy-datum. 
     ls_vbak-ernam = sy-uname. 
     ls_vbak-auart = ls_deep-auart. 
     ls_vbak-kunnr = ls_deep-kunnr. 
 
    MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_vbak TO ls_vbak2. 
 
    INSERT zdt_vbak FROM ls_vbak2. 
 
    CLEAR lv_posnr. 
 
    LOOP AT ls_deep-znavigation INTO DATA(ls_deep_item). 
       lv_posnr = lv_posnr + 10. 
 
      ls_vbap-vbeln  = lv_vbeln_n. 
       ls_vbap-posnr  = lv_posnr. 
       ls_vbap-matnr  = ls_deep_item-matnr. 
       ls_vbap-kwmeng = ls_deep_item-kwmeng. 
       ls_vbap-vrkme  = ls_deep_item-vrkme. 
 
      MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_vbap TO ls_vbap2. 
 
      INSERT zdt_vbap FROM ls_vbap2. 
 
      CLEAR ls_vbap2. 
     ENDLOOP. 
     ls_deep-vbeln = lv_vbeln_n. 
     ls_deep-erdat = sy-datum. 
 
    copy_data_to_ref( 
       EXPORTING 
         is_data = ls_deep 
       CHANGING 
         cr_data = er_deep_entity 
     ). 

  ENDMETHOD.

 

 UI5 Implementation:

Step 1: View – Binding Data and Preparing Navigation:

  • The view shows a list of sales orders by binding the table to the header entity (vbakSet). When the screen loads, it automatically fetches only basic data (like order number, date, customer). It does not load item data here, so the screen stays fast and simple. 
  • Each row in the table is made clickable and shows a small arrow because of the navigation type. This tells the user that clicking a row will take them to another screen. 
  • When a row is clicked, the view triggers an event and sends the selected row’s data to the controller. This includes the important key field (Vbeln), which is needed to identify the sales order. 
  • From a deep entity point of view, the view does not load full data. It only shows header data and prepares the key, so that the next screen can use it to fetch complete data (header + items) together. 

 

<mvc:View
    controllerName="project1.controller.View1"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:f="sap.f"
    displayBlock="true">

    <f:DynamicPage
        id="dynPage"
        toggleHeaderOnTitleClick="false">

        <!-- ================= TITLE ================= -->
        <f:title>
            <f:DynamicPageTitle>

                <f:heading>
                    <HBox alignItems="Center">
                        <Avatar
                            src="sap-icon://sales-order"
                            displayShape="Square"
                            class="sapUiTinyMarginEnd"/>

                        <VBox>
                            <Title text="Sales Orders" level="H2"/>
                            <Text text="Manage and track sales orders"/>
                        </VBox>
                    </HBox>
                </f:heading>

                <f:actions>
                    <Button
                        text="Create"
                        icon="sap-icon://add"
                        type="Emphasized"
                        press="onCreate"/>

                    <!--
                    <Button
                        text="Edit"
                        icon="sap-icon://edit"
                        press="onUpdate"
                        enabled="{= ${vbakTable>/selectedItems}.length === 1 }"/>
                    -->

                    <Button
                        text="Delete"
                        icon="sap-icon://delete"
                        type="Negative"
                        press="onDelete"
                        enabled="{= ${vbakTable>/selectedItems}.length > 0 }"/>
                </f:actions>

            </f:DynamicPageTitle>
        </f:title>

        <!-- ================= HEADER ================= -->
        <f:header>
            <f:DynamicPageHeader pinnable="true">

                <Toolbar design="Transparent">
                    <Label text="Search"/>
                    <SearchField
                        width="20rem"
                        liveChange="onSearch"
                        placeholder="Sales Order / Customer"/>
                </Toolbar>

            </f:DynamicPageHeader>
        </f:header>

        <!-- ================= CONTENT ================= -->
        <f:content>
            <Table
                id="vbakTable"
                items="{ path: '/vbakSet' }"
                growing="true"
                growingScrollToLoad="true"
                mode="MultiSelect"
                itemPress="onItemPress"
                sticky="ColumnHeaders"
                noDataText="No sales orders found"
                busyIndicatorDelay="0">

                <!-- ================= COLUMNS ================= -->
                <columns>
                    <Column width="11rem">
                        <Text text="Sales Order"/>
                    </Column>

                    <Column width="9rem" demandPopin="true" minScreenWidth="Tablet">
                        <Text text="Created On"/>
                    </Column>

                    <Column width="9rem" demandPopin="true" minScreenWidth="Tablet">
                        <Text text="Created By"/>
                    </Column>

                    <Column width="7rem">
                        <Text text="Type"/>
                    </Column>

                    <Column width="11rem" demandPopin="true" minScreenWidth="Desktop">
                        <Text text="Customer"/>
                    </Column>
                </columns>

                <!-- ================= ITEMS ================= -->
                <items>
                    <ColumnListItem
                        type="Navigation">

                        <cells>
                            <ObjectIdentifier
                                title="{Vbeln}"
                                titleActive="true"/>

                            <Text text="{Erdat}"/>

                            <Text text="{Ernam}"/>

                            <ObjectStatus
                                text="{Auart}"
                                state="Information"/>

                            <Text text="{Kunnr}"/>
                        </cells>

                    </ColumnListItem>
                </items>

            </Table>
        </f:content>

    </f:DynamicPage>
</mvc:View>

 

Step 2: Controller – Handling Row Selection and Parameter-Based Routing:

  • When the user clicks on a row, the controller takes that row and gets its data. From that data, it picks the Sales Order number (Vbeln), which is the main key. 
  • After getting this key, the controller performs router-based navigation using parameter-based (dynamic) routing. It sends the user to the Detail page and passes the Vbeln as a parameter, so the next screen knows exactly which sales order to load. 
  • In terms of deep entity, the controller is not loading all the data here. It is just sending the key, and using that key, the next screen will fetch full data (header + items) together. 

 

sap.ui.define(
    [
        "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller"
    ],
    function (Controller) {
        "use strict";

        return Controller.extend("project1.controller.View1", {

            onInit: function () {

            },

            onCreate: function () {
                this.getOwnerComponent().getRouter().navTo("Create");
            },

            onItemPress: function (oEvent) {
                debugger;

                var sVbeln = oEvent
                    .getParameter("listItem")
                    .getBindingContext()
                    .getProperty("Vbeln");

                this.getOwnerComponent().getRouter().navTo("Detail", {
                    Vbeln: sVbeln
                });
            }

        });
    }
);

 

 Output:

Step 3: View – Showing Header and Item Data: 

  • The view is designed to show one sales order in detail. It displays both the header data (like order number, customer, date) and the item data (list of materials). 
  • It uses a local model (detail) where data is divided into two parts: 
  • Header → main sales order 
  • Items → list of related items 
  • The header section is bound to detail>/Header, and the table is bound to detail>/Items, so it can show parent + child data together. 

 

<mvc:View
    controllerName="project1.controller.Detail"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:uxap="sap.uxap"
    displayBlock="true">

    <uxap:ObjectPageLayout
        id="ObjectPage"
        enableLazyLoading="true"
        showHeaderContent="true"
        showAnchorBar="true"
        upperCaseAnchorBar="false"
        isChildPage="true">

        <!-- ================= HEADER ================= -->
        <uxap:headerTitle>
            <uxap:ObjectPageHeader
                objectTitle="{detail>/Header/Vbeln}"
                objectSubtitle="Sales Order"
                objectImageURI="sap-icon://sales-order"
                objectImageShape="Square"
                isObjectIconAlwaysVisible="true">

                <!-- Header Actions (Fiori standard) -->
                <uxap:actions>
                    <Button
                        icon="sap-icon://nav-back"
                        type="Emphasized"
                        tooltip="Back"
                        press="onNavBack"/>

                    <Button
                        icon="sap-icon://edit"
                        text="Edit"
                        type="Attention"/>
                </uxap:actions>

            </uxap:ObjectPageHeader>
        </uxap:headerTitle>

        <!-- ================= HEADER CONTENT ================= -->
        <uxap:headerContent>
            <HBox
                justifyContent="Start"
                alignItems="Center"
                class="sapUiSmallMargin">

                <ObjectStatus
                    title="Document Type"
                    text="{detail>/Header/Auart}"
                    state="Information"/>

            </HBox>
        </uxap:headerContent>

        <!-- ================= SECTIONS ================= -->
        <uxap:sections>

            <!-- ================= GENERAL INFORMATION ================= -->
            <uxap:ObjectPageSection
                title="General Information"
                titleUppercase="false">

                <uxap:subSections>
                    <uxap:ObjectPageSubSection>

                        <VBox class="sapUiMediumMargin sapUiResponsiveContentPadding">

                            <HBox wrap="Wrap" class="sapUiSmallMarginBottom">

                                <VBox width="18rem" class="sapUiSmallMarginEnd">
                                    <Label text="Customer"/>
                                    <ObjectIdentifier title="{detail>/Header/Kunnr}"/>
                                </VBox>

                                <VBox width="18rem" class="sapUiSmallMarginEnd">
                                    <Label text="Created On"/>
                                    <Text text="{detail>/Header/Erdat}"/>
                                </VBox>

                                <VBox width="18rem">
                                    <Label text="Created By"/>
                                    <Text text="{detail>/Header/Ernam}"/>
                                </VBox>

                            </HBox>

                        </VBox>

                    </uxap:ObjectPageSubSection>
                </uxap:subSections>
            </uxap:ObjectPageSection>

            <!-- ================= ITEMS ================= -->
            <uxap:ObjectPageSection
                title="Items"
                titleUppercase="true">

                <uxap:subSections>
                    <uxap:ObjectPageSubSection>

                        <Table
                            items="{detail>/Items}"
                            growing="true"
                            growingScrollToLoad="true"
                            sticky="HeaderToolbar,ColumnHeaders"
                            class="sapUiResponsiveContentPadding">

                            <!-- Section Toolbar (Fiori pattern) -->
                            <headerToolbar>
                                <OverflowToolbar>
                                    <Title text="Order Items" level="H3"/>
                                    <ToolbarSpacer/>

                                    <Text text="Items: {= ${detail>/Items}.length }"/>

                                    <ToolbarSeparator/>

                                    <Button
                                        icon="sap-icon://add"
                                        text="Add Item"
                                        type="Transparent"/>
                                </OverflowToolbar>
                            </headerToolbar>

                            <columns>
                                <Column width="5rem">
                                    <Text text="Item"/>
                                </Column>

                                <Column>
                                    <Text text="Material"/>
                                </Column>

                                <Column hAlign="End" width="8rem">
                                    <Text text="Quantity"/>
                                </Column>

                                <Column width="6rem">
                                    <Text text="Unit"/>
                                </Column>
                            </columns>

                            <items>
                                <ColumnListItem>
                                    <cells>
                                        <ObjectIdentifier title="{detail>Posnr}"/>
                                        <Text text="{detail>Matnr}"/>
                                        <ObjectNumber number="{detail>Kwmeng}"/>
                                        <ObjectStatus text="{detail>Vrkme}"/>
                                    </cells>
                                </ColumnListItem>
                            </items>

                        </Table>

                    </uxap:ObjectPageSubSection>
                </uxap:subSections>
            </uxap:ObjectPageSection>

        </uxap:sections>

        <!-- ================= FOOTER (Fiori standard) ================= -->
        <uxap:footer>
            <OverflowToolbar>
                <ToolbarSpacer/>

                <Button
                    text="Edit"
                    icon="sap-icon://edit"
                    type="Emphasized"/>

                <Button
                    text="Cancel"
                    type="Transparent"
                    press="onNavBack"/>
            </OverflowToolbar>
        </uxap:footer>

    </uxap:ObjectPageLayout>
</mvc:View>

 

Step 4: Controller – Fetching Deep Entity Data:

  • The controller gets the Vbeln (Sales Order number) from routing when the screen opens. 
  • Using this key, it makes one OData call with $expand, which fetches: 
  • Header data 
  • Item data 

in a single request (deep entity). 

  • After getting the data, it splits it into: 
  • Header → stored separately 
  • Items → stored separately 

and sets it into the local model, which is then shown in the view. 

 

sap.ui.define(
    [
        "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller",
        "sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel"
    ],
    function (Controller, JSONModel) {
        "use strict";

        return Controller.extend("project1.controller.Detail", {

            onInit: function () {
                debugger;

                this.getOwnerComponent()
                    .getRouter()
                    .getRoute("Detail")
                    .attachPatternMatched(this._onObjectMatched, this);

                this.getView().setModel(
                    new JSONModel({
                        Header: {},
                        Items: []
                    }),
                    "detail"
                );
            },

            _onObjectMatched: function (oEvent) {
                debugger;

                var sVbeln = oEvent.getParameter("arguments").Vbeln;

                sVbeln = sVbeln.padStart(10, "0");

                var oModel = this.getOwnerComponent().getModel();
                var oDetailModel = this.getView().getModel("detail");

                oModel.read("/vbakSet('" + sVbeln + "')", {
                    urlParameters: {
                        "$expand": "znavigation"
                    },
                    success: function (oData) {
                        debugger;

                        oDetailModel.setProperty("/Header", oData);
                        oDetailModel.setProperty(
                            "/Items",
                            oData.znavigation.results
                        );
                    },
                    error: function () {
                        debugger;
                    }
                });
            },

            onNavBack: function () {
                this.getOwnerComponent()
                    .getRouter()
                    .navTo("RouteView1");
            }

        });
    }
);

 

 Output:

Step 5: View – Entering Header and Item Data:

  • The view is designed to create a new sales order. It allows the user to enter both header data (like document type, customer) and item data (materials, quantity, unit) in the same screen. 
  • It uses a local model (local) to store user input. The header fields are directly bound to this model, and the items table is bound to local>/Items, which holds multiple item entries. 
  • The user can dynamically add or remove items, so the view supports multiple child entries under one header. 

 

<mvc:View
    controllerName="project1.controller.Create"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:uxap="sap.uxap"
    xmlns:form="sap.ui.layout.form">

    <uxap:ObjectPageLayout
        id="CreateSalesOrderPage"
        showHeaderContent="true">

        <!-- ================= HEADER ================= -->
        <uxap:headerTitle>
            <uxap:ObjectPageHeader
                objectTitle="Create Sales Order"
                objectSubtitle="Header and Items"
                objectImageURI="sap-icon://sales-order"
                objectImageShape="Square">

                <uxap:actions>
                    <Button
                        text="Save"
                        icon="sap-icon://save"
                        type="Emphasized"
                        press="onSave"/>

                    <Button
                        text="Cancel"
                        icon="sap-icon://cancel"
                        type="Reject"
                        press="onCancel"/>
                </uxap:actions>

            </uxap:ObjectPageHeader>
        </uxap:headerTitle>

        <!-- ================= SECTIONS ================= -->
        <uxap:sections>

            <!-- ================= HEADER DATA ================= -->
            <uxap:ObjectPageSection title="Header Data">
                <uxap:subSections>
                    <uxap:ObjectPageSubSection>

                        <form:SimpleForm
                            editable="true"
                            layout="ResponsiveGridLayout"
                            labelSpanXL="3"
                            labelSpanL="3"
                            labelSpanM="4"
                            labelSpanS="12">

                            <Label text="Sales Document Type"/>
                            <Input
                                value="{local>/Auart}"
                                id="Aurat"
                                placeholder="AUART"
                                showValueHelp="true"
                                valueHelpRequest="onAuartValueHelp"/>

                            <Label text="Customer"/>
                            <Input
                                id="Kunnr"
                                value="{local>/Kunnr}"
                                placeholder="KUNNR"/>

                            <Label text="Created On"/>
                            <DatePicker editable="false"/>

                            <Label text="Created By"/>
                            <Input editable="false"/>

                        </form:SimpleForm>

                    </uxap:ObjectPageSubSection>
                </uxap:subSections>
            </uxap:ObjectPageSection>

            <!-- ================= ITEM DATA ================= -->
            <uxap:ObjectPageSection title="Items">
                <uxap:subSections>
                    <uxap:ObjectPageSubSection>

                        <Table
                            id="itemTable"
                            mode="MultiSelect"
                            growing="true"
                            items="{
                                path: 'local>/Items'
                            }">

                            <headerToolbar>
                                <Toolbar>
                                    <Title text="Sales Order Items"/>
                                    <ToolbarSpacer/>
                                    <Button
                                        icon="sap-icon://add"
                                        text="Add Item"
                                        press="onAddItem"/>
                                    <Button
                                        icon="sap-icon://delete"
                                        text="Remove Item"
                                        press="onRemoveItem"/>
                                </Toolbar>
                            </headerToolbar>

                            <columns>
                                <Column>
                                    <Text text="Item"/>
                                </Column>
                                <Column>
                                    <Text text="Material"/>
                                </Column>
                                <Column hAlign="End">
                                    <Text text="Quantity"/>
                                </Column>
                                <Column>
                                    <Text text="Unit"/>
                                </Column>
                            </columns>

                            <items>
                                <ColumnListItem>
                                    <cells>
                                        <Input
                                            value="{local>Posnr}"
                                            editable="false"/>

                                        <Input
                                            value="{local>Matnr}"
                                            placeholder="MATNR"/>

                                        <Input
                                            type="Number"
                                            value="{local>Kwmeng}"
                                            placeholder="Quantity"/>

                                        <Input
                                            value="{local>Vrkme}"
                                            placeholder="Unit"/>
                                    </cells>
                                </ColumnListItem>
                            </items>

                        </Table>

                    </uxap:ObjectPageSubSection>
                </uxap:subSections>
            </uxap:ObjectPageSection>

        </uxap:sections>

    </uxap:ObjectPageLayout>
</mvc:View>

 

Step 6:Creating Deep Entity (Header + Items):

  • The controller manages user input and stores all item data in the local model. When the user clicks save, it collects both header and item data.  
  • It creates a single payload that contains:  
  • Header data (Auart, Kunnr, etc.)  
  • Item data (array of items)  
  • Then it makes one create a call to backend, sending everything together.  
  • After successful creation, it reads the latest sales order and navigates to the Detail page using the generated Vbeln. 

 

sap.ui.define(
    [
        "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller",
        "sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel",
        "sap/m/MessageToast"
    ],
    function (Controller, JSONModel, MessageToast) {
        "use strict";

        return Controller.extend("project1.controller.Create", {

            onInit: function () {

                // Local JSON model for dynamic items
                var oData = {
                    Items: []
                };

                var oLocalModel = new JSONModel(oData);
                this.getView().setModel(oLocalModel, "local");
            },

            /* =========================================================== */
            /* NAVIGATION */
            /* =========================================================== */

            onCancel: function () {
                this.getOwnerComponent()
                    .getRouter()
                    .navTo("RouteView1");
            },

            /* =========================================================== */
            /* VALUE HELP */
            /* =========================================================== */

            oAuartVh: null,

            onAuartValueHelp: function () {

                if (!this.oAuartVh) {
                    this.oAuartVh = sap.ui.xmlfragment(
                        "project1.Fragments.AuartVH",
                        this
                    );

                    this.getView().addDependent(this.oAuartVh);
                }

                this.oAuartVh.open();
            },

            onAuartCancel: function () {
                this.oAuartVh.close();
            },

            onAuartConfirm: function (oEvent) {

                var oItem = oEvent.getParameter("selectedItem");
                if (!oItem) {
                    return;
                }

                this.byId("Aurat").setValue(oItem.getTitle());
            },

            onExit: function () {

                if (this.oAuartVh) {
                    this.oAuartVh.destroy();
                    this.oAuartVh = null;
                }
            },

            /* =========================================================== */
            /* ADD ITEM */
            /* =========================================================== */

            onAddItem: function () {

                var oModel = this.getView().getModel("local");
                var aItems = oModel.getProperty("/Items");

                aItems.push({
                    Posnr: "",
                    Matnr: "",
                    Kwmeng: "",
                    Vrkme: ""
                });

                oModel.setProperty("/Items", aItems);
            },

            /* =========================================================== */
            /* REMOVE ITEM */
            /* =========================================================== */

            onRemoveItem: function () {

                var oTable = this.byId("itemTable");
                var oModel = this.getView().getModel("local");
                var aItems = oModel.getProperty("/Items");

                var aSelectedItems = oTable.getSelectedItems();

                if (aSelectedItems.length === 0) {
                    MessageToast.show("Select at least one item to delete");
                    return;
                }

                aSelectedItems.forEach(function (oItem) {
                    var sPath = oItem
                        .getBindingContext("local")
                        .getPath();

                    var iIndex = parseInt(sPath.split("/")[2]);
                    aItems.splice(iIndex, 1);
                });

                oModel.setProperty("/Items", aItems);
            },

            /* =========================================================== */
            /* SAVE (DEEP ENTITY CREATE) */
            /* =========================================================== */

            onSave: function () {
                debugger;

                var oModel = this.getOwnerComponent().getModel();
                var aItems = this.getView()
                    .getModel("local")
                    .getProperty("/Items");

                if (aItems.length === 0) {
                    MessageToast.show("Please add at least one item");
                    return;
                }

                var oPayload = {
                    Auart: this.byId("Aurat").getValue(),
                    Kunnr: this.byId("Kunnr").getValue(),
                    Erdat: new Date().toISOString().split("T")[0],
                    znavigation: aItems
                };

                oModel.create("/vbakSet", oPayload, {

                    success: (oData) => {

                        this._readLatestOrder();
                        MessageToast.show("Sales Order Created");

                        // Clear header fields
                        this.byId("Aurat").setValue("");
                        this.byId("Kunnr").setValue("");

                        // Clear items
                        this.getView()
                            .getModel("local")
                            .setProperty("/Items", []);
                    },

                    error: (oError) => {
                        MessageToast.show("Error while creating Sales Order");
                    }
                });
            },

            /* =========================================================== */
            /* READ LATEST ORDER */
            /* =========================================================== */

            _readLatestOrder: function () {

                var oModel = this.getOwnerComponent().getModel();

                oModel.read("/vbakSet", {
                    urlParameters: {
                        "$orderby": "Vbeln desc"
                    },

                    success: function (oData) {

                        if (oData.results.length > 0) {
                            var sVbeln = oData.results[0].Vbeln;

                            this.getOwnerComponent()
                                .getRouter()
                                .navTo("Detail", {
                                    Vbeln: sVbeln
                                });
                        }

                    }.bind(this),

                    error: function () {
                        MessageToast.show("Read failed");
                    }
                });
            }

        });
    }
);

 

  • Here view collects full data (header + items)  
  • The controller sends full data in one call (deep insert)  
  • This completes the deep entity flow for create scenario 

Output:

  •  Conclusion

Deep Entity simplifies handling header and item data in a single request, reducing multiple OData calls and keeping both backend and UI logic clean.

In this blog, we covered the complete lifecycle:

  • Backend setup using SEGW
  • Data retrieval using $expand
  • Deep insert (header + items in one call)
  • UI5 integration.

entry.title: Re: SAP Solution Map by Company Size
entry.content:
To answer your question, how they differ  

  • GROW with SAP:
    • Targets companies new to ERP or upgrading from lightweight systems.
    • Emphasizes speed, simplicity, and standardized processes.
    • Ideal for organizations that want to adopt SAP best practices quickly without heavy customization.
  • RISE with SAP:
    • Aimed at enterprises with legacy landscapes and complex requirements.
    • Bundles infrastructure, migration tools, and business process transformation.
    • Provides flexibility to run on hyperscalers (Azure, AWS, GCP) or SAP’s own cloud.
  • If your organization is medium-sized and growing, GROW with SAP is the right fit — it’s cost-effective and accelerates ERP adoption.
  • If you’re a large enterprise with multiple geographies, legacy systems, and complex processes, RISE with SAP is the recommended path.

entry.title: Implementing Determination Using Association in SAP RAP
entry.content:
Introduction

In SAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP), determinations are a powerful mechanism that automatically derive or calculate field values based on changes to other fields within a business object. They play a central role in enforcing business logic, keeping data consistent, and reducing manual input errors.

While determinations are commonly implemented using direct field references within the same entity, a more advanced and often necessary pattern involves leveraging associations — navigational links between related business object nodes or external CDS entities. This approach becomes essential when the value you need to derive depends not on the current entity alone, but on data that lives in an associated entity.

This blog walks through the concept and practical implementation of determinations that use associations in SAP RAP.

I have implemented a scenario where the non-editable Total Amount field in the header entity is automatically determined based on the Price and Quantity fields maintained in the associated item entity.

Below is the Header entity, which includes the Total Amount field

 

Below is the item entity, which includes quantity and price  field

 

 

In the behavior definition, the Total Amount field is configured as non-editable using feature control, ensuring that it cannot be explicitly modified by the user.

 

Determination has been defined in the behavior definition of the Item entity.

The determination logic is implemented in the local handler class of the Item entity.

 

  METHOD calculate_total_price.

    DATA lt_header_update TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE zi_nk_header.

*Step 1 : Read changed items
    READ ENTITIES OF zi_nk_header IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY zi_nk_item
        FIELDS ( soid quantity price currency )
        WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
      RESULT DATA(lt_items)
      FAILED DATA(lf).

    DATA lt_header_keys TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE zi_nk_header.

    LOOP AT lt_items ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<item>).
*Step 2 :Navigate to parent headers using back-association
      READ ENTITIES OF zi_nk_header IN LOCAL MODE
        ENTITY zi_nk_item BY \_header
          FIELDS ( soid totalamount currency )
          WITH VALUE #( ( %tky = <item>-%tky ) )
        RESULT DATA(lt_header_result)
        FAILED DATA(lf_h).

      LOOP AT lt_header_result ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<hdr>).
*Step 3 : Read all items under those headers
        READ ENTITIES OF zi_nk_header IN LOCAL MODE
          ENTITY zi_nk_header BY \_salesitem
            FIELDS ( quantity price currency )
            WITH VALUE #( ( %tky = <hdr>-%tky ) )
          RESULT DATA(lt_all_items)
          FAILED DATA(lf2).

*Step 4 : Calculate the total amount
*Here i have used decfloat34 to avoid the loss of decimal values and incorrect totals

        DATA(lv_total) = REDUCE decfloat34(
          INIT sum = CONV decfloat34( '0' )
          FOR <it> IN lt_all_items
          NEXT sum = sum + ( <it>-quantity * <it>-price )
        ).

        APPEND VALUE #(
          %tky        = <hdr>-%tky
          totalamount = lv_total
          currency    = <item>-currency
          %control    = VALUE #( totalamount = if_abap_behv=>mk-on )
        ) TO lt_header_update.

      ENDLOOP.
    ENDLOOP.

*Step 5 : Update the header entity
    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_nk_header IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY zi_nk_header
        UPDATE FIELDS ( totalamount currency )
        WITH lt_header_update
      FAILED DATA(lf3)
      REPORTED DATA(lr).

  ENDMETHOD.

 

The Total Amount and Currency fields are rendered as read-only, preventing any direct user modification.

In the Item entity, users provide input for Quantity and Price, based on which the Total Amount is determined and automatically updated in the associated Header entity.

 

Output:-

 

Conclusion:-

This approach ensures data consistency, reduces redundancy, and keeps the business logic modular and maintainable. It is especially useful in scenarios like calculating header totals based on item data, where changes in one entity automatically reflect in another.

entry.title: SAP GRC Process Control Job Scheduling Issues – SAP Notes & Keywords Guide
entry.content:
1. Introduction

Automated Control Monitoring (ACM) jobs in SAP GRC Process Control often fail due to hidden technical, authorization, or configuration issues.

Instead of generic troubleshooting, this blog provides a keyword-driven SAP Notes mapping, allowing you to:

  • Search error text directly
  • Identify exact SAP Note
  • Fix issues faster

2. SAP Notes + Exact Keywords + Symptoms Mapping

🔴 A. Job Not Triggering / Scheduling Issues

SAP Note Accuracy Exact Keywords (Search Terms) Symptoms

359305295%"job not triggered", "ACM job not starting", "background job scheduled not running"Job remains in Scheduled status
332805088%"scheduler not picking job", "job not executed"Job not picked by scheduler
331209789%"job skipped", "ACM job intermittent execution"Job skipped randomly
324887091%"job scheduling issue no error", "silent failure job"No logs, no execution
291987992%"job not created", "ACM scheduling failure"Job missing in SM37
291140694%"start condition not met", "job condition issue"Job never starts

🔴 B. Job Execution Failures / Dumps

SAP NoteAccuracyExact Keywords (Search Terms)Symptoms
342681996%"short dump OBJECTS_OBJREF_NOT_ASSIGNED", "null reference ACM", "ST22 dump ACM job"Dump during execution
370117589%"runtime exception ACM job", "job cancelled runtime error"Job terminated abruptly
369530785%"job stopped mid execution", "incomplete job log"Execution stops midway
369402083%"unexpected termination control evaluation"Job fails unpredictably
369348286%"internal table inconsistency dump", "ITAB error ACM"Dump due to table issue
356574989%"method execution failed", "class error ACM job"Class-level execution error
352533790%"runtime error background job", "ACM job failure"Job fails in processing
344682693%"dump in CL_GRRM*", "ACM class dump"Dump in execution class
296865687%"job cancelled", "technical exception ACM"Job cancelled

🔴 C. Data Fetch / Mapping Issues

SAP NoteAccuracyExact Keywords (Search Terms)Symptoms
361501892%"missing rs_category_id", "rs_category_title not populated", "ACM missing fields", "CL_GRRM_MODEL_CRISK READ_CRISK issue"Missing fields in report
361274984%"incorrect data fetch ACM", "wrong values in output"Incorrect data
357250793%"data inconsistency ACM tables", "report mismatch data"Data mismatch
355561492%"mapping issue blank fields", "ACM empty fields"Blank fields
355336295%"partial data fetched", "ACM incomplete data"Partial results
352186695%"missing control attributes", "control data missing ACM"Missing attributes
349573492%"data mismatch source vs result", "ACM inconsistent output"Data mismatch

🔴 D. Incorrect Results / Report Issues

SAP NoteAccuracyExact Keywords (Search Terms)Symptoms
338118190%"incorrect control evaluation", "wrong ACM results"Incorrect results
258216095%"report output mismatch", "ACM report incorrect"Output mismatch
240150793%"inconsistent results execution", "ACM result variation"Different results
257382095%"risk control mapping incorrect", "wrong mapping ACM"Mapping issue
238812291%"missing report entries", "ACM missing records"Missing entries
264394293%"incorrect display ACM report", "UI data issue"Display issue

🔴 E. Authorization Issues

SAP NoteAccuracyExact Keywords (Search Terms)Symptoms
337034993%"authorization error ACM job", "no authorization background user"Job fails due to auth
206494491%"user cannot execute control", "authorization missing ACM"Execution blocked
201083191%"missing authorization object GRC", "ACM auth issue"Auth failure

🔴 F. Master Data / Configuration Issues

SAP NoteAccuracyExact Keywords (Search Terms)Symptoms
284621894%"controls not picked ACM", "no controls executed"Controls missing
190937393%"org hierarchy issue GRC", "organizational mapping incorrect"Hierarchy issue
212993094%"control assignment missing", "ACM config issue"Missing assignments
195405490%"configuration inconsistency ACM", "setup issue job"Config issue
219957494%"invalid master data ACM", "data inconsistency config"Invalid data

🔴 G. Performance / Miscellaneous

SAP NoteAccuracyExact Keywords (Search Terms)Symptoms
363037587%"ACM job performance issue", "long running job", "slow job execution"Performance issue

3. How to Use This Effectively

  1. Copy error text from:
    • Job log (SM37)
    • Dump (ST22)
    • Application log (SLG1)
  2. Search using exact keywords above
  3. Identify matching SAP Note
  4. Implement fix via SNOTE

4. Real Case Mapping (Your Scenario)

Issue:
Risk category fields not coming

Keywords:

  • "rs_category_id not populated"
  • "rs_category_title missing"
  • "CL_GRRM_MODEL_CRISK READ_CRISK issue"

SAP Note: 3615018

5. Conclusion

Using SAP Notes + exact keywords + symptoms mapping provides a fast, reliable way to troubleshoot ACM job scheduling issues in SAP GRC Process Control.

Hint:
#SAPGRC #ProcessControl #ACM #SAPNotes #ErrorHandling
@SAPSupport @GRCExperts @ABAPDevelopers

entry.title: Wrong Assignment of Purchasing group to company in SRM PPOCA_BBP
entry.content: Wrong assignment has been done in PPOCA_BBP tcode during new Purchase group creation. A new Purchase group has been create and wrongly map the highest organization under Purchasing group. Purchasing group is AD4, under which NDPL Org is mapped . Need to delink the AD4 and NDPL linkage and  AD4 should be assinged to below NDPL-HOD Contract-HOG2-EN3.

entry.title: Re: CM01 not pulling in unplanned orders
entry.content: OPA2 controls which categories of capacity requirements (planned orders, production orders) are considered in CM01, while OPU3 defines the scheduling parameters and which statuses of those orders (created, released, etc.) are relevant for capacity planning. So if you only see released production orders in CM01, it is because the OPU3 settings tied to your profile restrict capacity requirements to released status. To see all demand, your SAP team needs to adjust OPU3 so that “created” or “unreleased” production orders are also included, and then ensure your OPA2 profile references both planned and production orders.

entry.title: Re: Cloud ALM User
entry.content:
This perspective is absolutely accurate and gets to the heart of a sustainable IT strategy. The key question of where the actual work and knowledge building should take place is crucial for long-term success.

From a pragmatic point of view, Option 3 is the most future-proof approach, as it focuses on enablement and building internal capacity.

The limitations of projects: Project structures (as in Option 2) are inherently temporary. After go-live or the completion of an implementation, the project team disbands. Without targeted, early knowledge transfer, a critical support vacuum arises at precisely this point.

The necessity of internal operations: Especially with mission-critical and highly complex architectures (such as S/4HANA landscapes, extensive NetWeaver stacks, or Pacemaker clusters), the parent organization is absolutely dependent on this knowledge. Smooth operation, troubleshooting, and patch management require a deep understanding of the systems, which should not be covered permanently or exclusively by external resources.

The strategic value: By empowering its own organization, the company becomes independent. A short-term project result is thus transformed into a long-term, resilient operational capability.

entry.title: Re: Cost Difference Not Posted Automatically at Subcontra...
entry.content: It is not possible in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to automatically post the cost difference at subcontracting GR. The standard approach is to use Material Ledger / Actual Costing at period-end. Without ML, the behavior you observed (inventory at standard cost, no variance posting) is exactly how the system is designed.

Note that even if the Solution Manager starts transactions in managed systems, you have to enter connection data for the Solution Manager system itself: the SAP GUI transaction always starts first in the Solution Manager system and then uses additional information to navigate to the required managed system.

entry.title: Preserving Critical Supplier Information: Transferring of Supplier Material Number (IDNLF)
entry.content:
Preserving Critical Supplier Information:

Why Transferring the Supplier Material Number (IDNLF) from Purchase Requisition to Purchase Order Matters

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, procurement processes are designed to be efficient, guided, and seamlessly integrated. From purchase requisition creation to purchase order execution, data consistency is key to ensuring smooth operational flows.

However, in everyday procurement scenarios, even small gaps in data transfer can lead to unnecessary effort, loss of information, and reduced user confidence. One such gap exists today in the handling of the Supplier Material Number (IDNLF) during the transition from Purchase Requisition (PR) to Purchase Order (PO).

________________________________________________________________________________________________________

The Business Scenario: A Common, Real‑World Process

Let’s look at a typical procurement flow:

  1. A Purchase Requisition is created in Manage Purchase Requisitions – Professional

    • A Desired Supplier is maintained
    • A Supplier Material Number (IDNLF) is entered at item level

  2. The requisition is processed in Process Purchase Requisitions

    • A Source of Supply is assigned (matching the Desired Supplier)

  3. A Purchase Order is created using Create Purchase Order

At this point, users expect all relevant and already‑maintained information to be transferred consistently.
However, the resulting Purchase Order does not contain the Supplier Material Number (IDNLF).

 

________________________________________________________________________________________________________

The Gap Today: Information Is Lost Along the Process

The Supplier Material Number is not a cosmetic field—it is often a business‑critical identifier used by suppliers for:

  • Order confirmation
  • Logistics and delivery processing
  • Invoice matching and clarification
  • Communication with supplier purchasing departments

When this field is entered manually in the Purchase Requisition but not transferred to the Purchase Order, important information is lost along an otherwise integrated process.

As a result:

  • Users need to re‑enter data manually
  • The risk of inconsistencies or errors increases
  • Additional clarification with suppliers becomes necessary

This breaks the promise of an end‑to‑end digital procurement process.

________________________________________________________________________________________________________

Why This Matters for Procurement Efficiency

In productive environments, Purchase Requisition processors handle high volumes of requisitions under time pressure. Every additional step reduces efficiency and increases frustration.

The missing transfer of IDNLF leads to:

  • Extra manual steps during PO creation or follow‑up changes
  • Reduced process speed
  • Potential quality issues in supplier communication

Instead of focusing on value‑adding tasks, users are forced to compensate for system limitations.

________________________________________________________________________________________________________

The Opportunity: Small Enhancement, High Value

The proposed improvement is straightforward:

Automatically transfer the Supplier Material Number (IDNLF) from the Purchase Requisition to the Purchase Order when a matching Source of Supply is assigned.

This ensures that:

  • All relevant procurement data is preserved throughout the process
  • The Purchase Order is complete from the moment it is created
  • Manual rework is eliminated

Technically simple, but with a disproportionately positive impact on daily procurement operations.

________________________________________________________________________________________________________

Expected Benefits

By closing this gap, SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud customers benefit from:

  • Improved efficiency and productivity
    Purchase Requisition processors can order faster with fewer steps in between.

  • Enhanced user experience
    Users trust that entered data is reliably carried forward.

  • Higher data quality
    Ensuring that no critical information is lost along the process.

  • Better supplier communication
    Suppliers receive Purchase Orders containing their own material references.

________________________________________________________________________________________________________

Call to Action: Support the Improvement Request

This improvement request is based on real customer usage. If you encounter similar challenges or agree that procurement data should remain consistent from requisition to order, I encourage you to:
