entry.title: Re: No Mass Upload Option for Standard Text App – Possibl...
entry.content:
Good day @MarkusDaum 

Thank you for your question.

Below are answers to them.

Question 1: Is there any standard approach for mass uploading standard texts?

Answer: No, the Public Cloud does not provide this functionality. That app is centered on interactive maintenance in the Fiori app rather than a bulk import process.

Question 2: Are there recommended tools, reports, or APIs to handle this?

Answer: Migration Cockpit is the only standard tool for your business data migration, and it works with predefined migration objects.

Question 3: Has anyone implemented a custom solution (e.g. via LSMW, BAPI, or ABAP program)?

Answer: Considering its Public Cloud, the classic ECC or on-premise approach is not the right pattern. As you know LSMW is restricted and not recommended for S4H migrations, and for Public Cloud, it supported migration path as mentioned in the previous answer is the Migration Cockpit with predefined objects. In addition to that, the developer extensibility model SAP provides in Public Cloud is limited to a 3-system landscape and to released development objects and APIs. So, a classic unrestricted ABAP upload report or LSMW style loader is out of the recommended standard.

What that practically means for you is:

  • Manual maintenance of standard texts must be done in the app.
  • You can use standard texts in supported business scenarios, e.g., output related processes. See KBA 3524757 for more details.
  • You can carry out custom development that sits within the Public Cloud supported solutions.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Product Substitution :Controlling Substitution Priori...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

Here's what our internal chatbot replied. Can you please check whether it helps you resolve the issue?

Product Substitution provides a general overview of the existing functionality.

To achieve the required substitution logic where Product A is substituted by Products B, C, and D in a specific sequence, you can configure the substitution strategy using the following approach:

  1. Define Substitution Strategy: Supply Chain - Configuring the Optimal Substitution Method

    • Use the ‘Configure Substitution Strategy’ app to define a substitution strategy that specifies the sequence of substitutions. You can set Product B as the first substitute, followed by Product C, and finally Product D.

  2. Sequence Configuration: Supply Chain - Combining Plant and Product Substitution

    • Ensure that the sequence values are set correctly in the substitution master data. This will ensure that the system checks Product B first, then Product C, and finally Product D.

  3. Handling Stock Availability: Supply Chain - Product Substitution Data Retrieval Logic

    • For Product D, you can configure the system to always confirm the quantity regardless of stock availability. This can be done by setting a rule in the substitution strategy that overrides the availability check for Product D.

  4. Batch Determination:

    • After material confirmation, the system can determine the batch based on FIFO rules, which is standard behavior.

  5. Alternative Approach:

    • If the standard functionality does not fully meet your requirements, consider using custom logic or enhancements to achieve the desired behavior. The following API is available: Product Substitution.

Kind regards, Henrike

 

 

 

 

#AI-Generated Content

entry.title: Re: Vendor Details for RE document
entry.content:
FAGLL03H and FBL3N are GL-focused reports. They show postings at the G/L account level, but they don’t always carry the vendor/customer master data fields unless the posting itself was made directly to a vendor account. For RE documents, the system posts to reconciliation accounts or adjustment accounts, not directly to the vendor master. That’s why vendor details don’t appear in FAGLL03H or FBL3N. If you need to analyze vendor information for those postings, you should use vendor line item reports (like FBL1N) or drill down from the GL line item to the original FI document, where the vendor master data is stored.

If your business needs vendor details to show up in FAGLL03H/FBL3N for RE documents, you’d need a custom enhancement (e.g., adding vendor fields via coding block extension or report modification).

entry.title: Re: If the tax rate changes in an already created PO. The...
entry.content:
When you create a PO with a non‑deductible tax code, the system calculates the non‑deductible tax amount (EKPO‑NAVNW) at that point in time. Even if you later change the tax rate in the PO, the NAVNW field is not automatically re‑updated for valuation purposes. At Goods Receipt (MIGO), inventory is posted using the original NAVNW value (based on the tax rate at PO creation), which explains why your GRN valuation is still reflecting the 9% tax amount instead of the revised 5%.

This is not a bug; it is the intended design. To ensure correct valuation, SAP recommends either:

  • Re‑create the PO after tax rate changes, so NAVNW is recalculated.
  • Use Material Ledger / Actual Costing to adjust variances at period end.

If re‑creation is not feasible, a custom enhancement (e.g., BAdI for GR posting) would be needed to force recalculation of NAVNW at GR.

  • 3330262 - SQL Statement Collection: "HANA_Memory_Overview" report for SAP HANA
  • 3421144 - SQL Statement Collection: "HANA_Tables_DataCollector" report for SAP HANA
  • 3604090 - How to check historic information like table records, disk or memory size for certain table

Regards,
Laszlo

entry.title: Partner Determination Not Triggered in PO (ME21N) – EKPA Empty Despite Correct Configuration
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I seek your guidance on an issue related to partner determination in Purchase Orders.

System Context:

  • SAP ECC (MM – Purchasing)
  • Standard transaction: ME21N / ME23N
  • Custom document type: ZSO / ZCO
  • Partner schema assigned: Z002

Configuration Details:

  • Partner schema Z002 is correctly assigned to the PO document types (verified via OMEC)
  • Schema contains mandatory partner roles:
    • VN (Vendor)
    • PI (Invoicing Party)
    • OA (Ordering Address)

Issue Description:
While creating a Purchase Order using a specific vendor (e.g., 100173):

  • The Partners tab remains completely blank
  • No partner roles are automatically populated
  • Upon checking table EKPA, no entries are created for the PO
  • However, the PO is still saved successfully without errors

Observations:

  1. Configuration is consistent:
    • Partner schema assignment is correct
    • Mandatory roles are defined in schema

  2. Vendor master (WYT3) contains partner entries such as:

    • LF (Vendor)
    • BA (Goods Supplier)
    • RS (Invoicing-related role)

    However, roles like PI and OA are not explicitly maintained.

  3. No custom developments, BAPI, or interface is involved in PO creation (manual ME21N)

Expected Behavior:
System should determine and populate partner roles (VN, PI, OA) in the PO based on vendor master and schema.

Actual Behavior:

  • Partner determination does not trigger
  • EKPA remains empty
  • Partners tab shows no data

Clarification Required:

  1. Does partner determination strictly require exact partner function codes (e.g., PI, OA) in vendor master, or can equivalent roles (e.g., RS, BA) be considered?
  2. In cases where mandatory roles are missing in vendor master, should the system:
    • Prevent PO creation?
    • Or allow PO creation but skip partner determination?
  3. Is there any standard SAP behavior where partner determination silently fails without error if required roles are not found?

Any insights or similar experiences would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Ayaz Khan

entry.title: Re: Inventory Packaging Materials Set-up guidance
entry.content:
Good day @Kevin12 

Thank you for your question.

Based on what you described, the experience is expected when you only use the packaging material to create the handling unit during packing, but no packaging item is generated in the outbound delivery.

Note that the system does not simply reduce the packaging stock simply because you created a HU in Pack Outbound Deliveries. For the packaging material to be consumed at PGI, the system must create a delivery item for the packaging material. You must also define the storage bin from which the packaging material is taken in Determine Goods Movement Bin.

KBA 2818830 explicitly refers to the creation of an Automatically Generated Packaging Item during goods issue in EWM. If you do not properly create the packaging item, GI related issues will occur.

Regarding your statement “e really need further instructions on how to configure the inventory to be consumed on Outbound Deliveries/PGI”, here are areas you should focus on:

  1. To ensure packaging inventory is consume on outbound delivery/PGI, you must configure packaging item generation. If you only pack with material PCKINVT in the work center and no packaging item is generated in the outbound delivery, then PGI will reduce the finished goods but not the packaging stock.
  2. At minimum you need to validate these points:
    1. Your packaging product type VERP is correct in principle, but VERP in Public Cloud depends on the relevant EWM scope activation. See KBA 2807296 for more details.
    2. You must generate delivery items for packaging materials. This requirement is the central requirement.
    3. As mentioned above, you must define the storage bin from which the packaging material is taken. See KBA 2818830 for more insights.
  3. Based on your description, the most likely reason for the packaging material not being reduced on the Post Goods Issue is this:
    • PCKINVT is available as a packaging material in the packing app
    • The system creates the HU
    • But the system does not create a consumable packaging delivery item
    • Therefore, PGI posts only the goods issue for the packed product, but not for PCKINVT.

Finally, as a next step, you should check the outbound delivery item list after packing, verify whether PCKINVT appears as a packaging item, and then review Determine Goods Movement Bin in your configuration. If no packaging item is generated, then that is the main reason your inventory is not consumed during PGI. This is what KBA 2818830 points to.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Partner Determination Not Triggered in PO (ME21N) – E...
entry.content:
How Partner determination works is the partner schema (Z002) defines which partner functions must be present in the PO (VN, PI, OA, etc.). At PO creation, the system looks into the vendor master partner functions (table WYT3) to pull the corresponding roles. If the vendor master does not contain the exact partner functions defined in the schema, the system cannot populate them in the PO.

In your case, Vendor master has LF, BA, RS. Schema requires VN, PI, OA. Since PI and OA are missing in the vendor master, the system cannot determine them. Hence, the result is EKPA stays empty, Partners tab is blank. SAP standard does not throw an error at PO creation; it allows saving even if mandatory schema roles are not found. The “mandatory” flag only enforces that the field must be filled if the partner function is present, not that the system blocks creation if it cannot be determined.

Maintain the missing partner functions (PI, OA) in the vendor master (Purchasing view → Partner functions). Or adjust the partner schema to align with the roles actually maintained in vendor master. If you want the system to block PO creation when mandatory partners are missing, you’d need a custom enhancement (e.g., user exit in ME21N/ME22N).
